title: Container images not detected after 'buildah bud' on Kubernetes
body:
**Description**
I am trying to use the `buildah/stable:v1.19.6` container to build and push a container image from within a Kubernetes `Job`. When I run `buildah bud` the output seems to indicate that the image is pulled and tagged locally but when `buildah images` is run immediately afterwards no images are reported. This means that a further call to `buildah push` fails due to unknown image ID.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Execute the chart found at github.com/aetherith/helm-container-build with the rendered result below. Sensitive information replaced with `<REDACTED>` and image namespace with `example`.

**Edited per comment below**
```yaml
---
# Source: container-build/templates/serviceaccount.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: example-container-build
  labels:
    helm.sh/chart: container-build-0.1.0
    app.kubernetes.io/name: container-build
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: example
    app.kubernetes.io/version: "v1.19.6"
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
---
# Source: container-build/templates/registryAuthSecret.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: example-container-build-source-auth
  labels:
    helm.sh/chart: container-build-0.1.0
    app.kubernetes.io/name: container-build
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: example
    app.kubernetes.io/version: "v1.19.6"
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
type: kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson
stringData:
  .dockerconfigjson: |
    {
      "auths": {
        "https://docker.io": {
          "username": "<REDACTED>",
          "password": "<REDACTED>",
          "auth": "<REDACTED>"
        }
      }
    }
---
# Source: container-build/templates/registryAuthSecret.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: example-container-build-dest-auth
  labels:
    helm.sh/chart: container-build-0.1.0
    app.kubernetes.io/name: container-build
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: example
    app.kubernetes.io/version: "v1.19.6"
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
stringData:
  .dockerconfigjson: |
    {
      "auths": {
        "https://docker.io": {
          "username": "<REDACTED>",
          "password": "<REDACTED>",
          "auth": "<REDACTED>"
        }
      }
    }
---
# Source: container-build/templates/configmap.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: example-container-build
  labels:
    helm.sh/chart: container-build-0.1.0
    app.kubernetes.io/name: container-build
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: example
    app.kubernetes.io/version: "v1.19.6"
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
data:
  Dockerfile: |
    FROM docker.io/library/ubuntu:20.10
---
# Source: container-build/templates/job.yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: example-container-build
  labels:
    helm.sh/chart: container-build-0.1.0
    app.kubernetes.io/name: container-build
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: example
    app.kubernetes.io/version: "v1.19.6"
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
spec:
  template:
    metadata:
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: example-container-build
      securityContext:
        {}
      containers:
        - name: container-build
          securityContext:
            {}
          image: "quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.19.6"
          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
          command:
            - "/usr/bin/bash"
            - "-cx"
          args:
            - >
              buildah bud
              --file Dockerfile
              --authfile /root/source-auth
              --storage-driver vfs
              --format docker
              --tag "example/ubuntu:latest"
              --debug
              /build
              &&
              buildah images --all
              &&
              buildah push
              --authfile /root/destination-auth
              "example/ubuntu:latest"
              docker://docker.io/example/ubuntu:latest
          volumeMounts:
            - name: cache
              mountPath: /var/lib/containers
            - name: build
              mountPath: /build
            - name: config
              mountPath: /build/Dockerfile
              subPath: Dockerfile
            - name: source-auth
              mountPath: "/root/source-auth"
              subPath: source-auth
              readOnly: true
            - name: destination-auth
              mountPath: "/root/destination-auth"
              subPath: destination-auth
              readOnly: true
          resources:
            {}
      volumes:
        - name: cache
          emptyDir: {}
        - name: build
          emptyDir: {}
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: example-container-build
        - name: source-auth
          secret:
            secretName: example-container-build-source-auth
            items:
              - key: .dockerconfigjson
                path: source-auth
        - name: destination-auth
          secret:
            secretName: example-container-build-dest-auth
            items:
              - key: .dockerconfigjson
                path: destination-auth
      restartPolicy: Never
```
 
**Expected Result**
The image is built and uploaded to DockerHub. This sequence of commands seems to work as expected on a rootless Fedora 33 install of `buildah`.

**Actual Result**
The image is downloaded and reports that it is tagged correctly, however `buildah images` does not report it existing and `buildah push` cannot push the image. 

comments.body:
**EDIT**
Found part of the problem, the `cache` volumeMount was mounting to `/var/containers` as opposed to `/var/lib/containers`. The same behavior exists though with the modification.

You need to use the same storage driver for each command.

```
$ buildah images --storage-driver=vfs --all
REPOSITORY   TAG   IMAGE ID   CREATED   SIZE

$ buildah images --storage-driver=overlay --all
REPOSITORY                          TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED        SIZE
docker.io/google/cloud-sdk          alpine   93d9655fad34   5 days ago     582 MB
<none>                              <none>   dca776f7ab4d   12 days ago    647 MB
docker.io/library/busybox           latest   a9d583973f65   12 days ago    1.45 MB
<none>                              <none>   00eed65879df   13 days ago    5.89 MB
<none>                              <none>   02575cd40605   2 weeks ago    217 MB
<none>                              <none>   5fa4d2e1e403   2 weeks ago    217 MB
<none>                              <none>   f35547fbbd09   2 weeks ago    217 MB
<none>                              <none>   e21f991a49c0   2 weeks ago    5.89 MB
<none>                              <none>   0e615b0166fc   2 weeks ago    5.89 MB
docker.io/jess/chromium             latest   ddf544b1c7f4   2 weeks ago    764 MB
docker.io/library/golang            latest   f15d23d96763   3 weeks ago    883 MB
localhost/test                      latest   28f6e2705743   4 weeks ago    5.88 MB
docker.io/library/alpine            latest   28f6e2705743   4 weeks ago    5.88 MB
<none>                              <none>   02480aeb44d7   4 weeks ago    5.62 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8     latest   b47c9053389d   5 weeks ago    245 MB
k8s.gcr.io/pause                    3.4.1    0f8457a4c2ec   2 months ago   690 KB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora   latest   9f2a56037643   2 months ago   182 MB
registry.centos.org/centos          latest   2f3766df23b6   3 months ago   217 MB
docker.io/library/golang            1.13     d6f3656320fe   7 months ago   824 MB
```
@rhatdan Thanks a ton! That fixed my issue immediately. I knew I was misunderstanding the tool somehow!
